steps.title: Substitute the linear equation into the quadratic
steps.content:
From the second equation, $y = -6x + 36$. Substitute into $x^2 = 6x + y$:
$$x^2 = 6x + (-6x + 36)$$
The $6x$ and $-6x$ cancel:
$$x^2 = 36$$

steps.title: Compute xy for each solution
steps.content:
Use $y = -6x + 36$:
If $x = 6$: $y = -36 + 36 = 0$, so $xy = 6 \cdot 0 = 0$.
If $x = -6$: $y = 36 + 36 = 72$, so $xy = -6 \cdot 72 = -432$.
The possible values of $xy$ are $0$ and $-432$.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) 0: Matches the solution $(6, 0)$ since $6 \cdot 0 = 0$.
  • B) 6: Neither solution yields $xy = 6$.
  • C) 12: Neither solution yields $xy = 12$.
  • D) 36: Neither solution yields $xy = 36$.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
